title: Roll over the hill
intro: This hill IS a transistor. The crowd of jittering balls is thermal electrons at the source; the hilltop is the channel barrier the gate controls; every ball that crests it rains into the drain — the arrival counter is literally the drain current. This device's gate is so short the drain has stolen the barrier: it leaks even when 'off'. Stretch the gate longer until the hill actually blocks the crowd: at least 10,000× more arrivals ON than OFF.
explain: The balls just taught you the Boltzmann tail: the fraction of a thermal crowd with energy above a barrier Eb is exp(−Eb/kT), so every kT·ln10 ≈ 59.6 meV of extra barrier cuts arrivals 10×. That exponential is the entire off-state of every chip you own. And you met short-channel effects from the terrain side: below ~30 nm a planar gate loses grip, the drain erodes the hilltop, and the off-floor rises exponentially. Fun fact: at the real '5nm' node the gate is ~18 nm long (IRDS 2021) — node names stopped being dimensions around 2011.

choices: A few more balls make it
Ten times or more — it explodes

prediction.prompt: If the gate drops the barrier by just 60 mV (half a hilltop notch), how many more balls get over?
